speaker: ORLANDO
text: Who’s there?

speaker: ADAM
text: What, my young master, O my gentle master,

speaker: ADAM
text: O my sweet master, O you memory

speaker: ADAM
text: Of old Sir Rowland! Why, what make you here?

speaker: ADAM
text: Why are you virtuous? Why do people love you?

speaker: ADAM
text: And wherefore are you gentle, strong, and valiant?

speaker: ADAM
text: Why would you be so fond to overcome

speaker: ADAM
text: The bonny prizer of the humorous duke?

speaker: ADAM
text: Your praise is come too swiftly home before you.

speaker: ADAM
text: Know you not, master, to some kind of men

speaker: ADAM
text: Their graces serve them but as enemies?

speaker: ADAM
text: No more do yours. Your virtues, gentle master,

speaker: ADAM
text: Are sanctified and holy traitors to you.

speaker: ADAM
text: O, what a world is this when what is comely

speaker: ADAM
text: Envenoms him that bears it!

speaker: ORLANDO
text: Why, what’s the matter?

speaker: ADAM
text: O unhappy youth,

speaker: ADAM
text: Come not within these doors. Within this roof

speaker: ADAM
text: The enemy of all your graces lives.

speaker: ADAM
text: Your brother—no, no brother—yet the son—

speaker: ADAM
text: Yet not the son, I will not call him son—

speaker: ADAM
text: Of him I was about to call his father,

speaker: ADAM
text: Hath heard your praises, and this night he means

speaker: ADAM
text: To burn the lodging where you use to lie,

speaker: ADAM
text: And you within it. If he fail of that,

speaker: ADAM
text: He will have other means to cut you off.

speaker: ADAM
text: I overheard him and his practices.

speaker: ADAM
text: This is no place, this house is but a butchery.

speaker: ADAM
text: Abhor it, fear it, do not enter it.

speaker: ORLANDO
text: Why, whither, Adam, wouldst thou have me go?

speaker: ADAM
text: No matter whither, so you come not here.

speaker: ORLANDO
text: What, wouldst thou have me go and beg my food,

speaker: ORLANDO
text: Or with a base and boist’rous sword enforce

speaker: ORLANDO
text: A thievish living on the common road?

speaker: ORLANDO
text: This I must do, or know not what to do;

speaker: ORLANDO
text: Yet this I will not do, do how I can.

speaker: ORLANDO
text: I rather will subject me to the malice

speaker: ORLANDO
text: Of a diverted blood and bloody brother.

speaker: ADAM
text: But do not so. I have five hundred crowns,

speaker: ADAM
text: The thrifty hire I saved under your father,

speaker: ADAM
text: Which I did store to be my foster nurse

speaker: ADAM
text: When service should in my old limbs lie lame,

speaker: ADAM
text: And unregarded age in corners thrown.

speaker: ADAM
text: Take that, and He that doth the ravens feed,

speaker: ADAM
text: Yea, providently caters for the sparrow,

speaker: ADAM
text: Be comfort to my age. Here is the gold.

speaker: ADAM
text: All this I give you. Let me be your servant.

speaker: ADAM
text: Though I look old, yet I am strong and lusty,

speaker: ADAM
text: For in my youth I never did apply

speaker: ADAM
text: Hot and rebellious liquors in my blood,

speaker: ADAM
text: Nor did not with unbashful forehead woo

speaker: ADAM
text: The means of weakness and debility.

speaker: ADAM
text: Therefore my age is as a lusty winter,

speaker: ADAM
text: Frosty but kindly. Let me go with you.

speaker: ADAM
text: I’ll do the service of a younger man

speaker: ADAM
text: In all your business and necessities.

speaker: ORLANDO
text: O good old man, how well in thee appears

speaker: ORLANDO
text: The constant service of the antique world,

speaker: ORLANDO
text: When service sweat for duty, not for meed.

speaker: ORLANDO
text: Thou art not for the fashion of these times,

speaker: ORLANDO
text: Where none will sweat but for promotion,

speaker: ORLANDO
text: And having that do choke their service up

speaker: ORLANDO
text: Even with the having. It is not so with thee.

speaker: ORLANDO
text: But, poor old man, thou prun’st a rotten tree

speaker: ORLANDO
text: That cannot so much as a blossom yield

speaker: ORLANDO
text: In lieu of all thy pains and husbandry.

speaker: ORLANDO
text: But come thy ways. We’ll go along together,

speaker: ORLANDO
text: And ere we have thy youthful wages spent,

speaker: ORLANDO
text: We’ll light upon some settled low content.

speaker: ADAM
text: Master, go on, and I will follow thee

speaker: ADAM
text: To the last gasp with truth and loyalty.

speaker: ADAM
text: From seventeen years till now almost fourscore

speaker: ADAM
text: Here livèd I, but now live here no more.

speaker: ADAM
text: At seventeen years, many their fortunes seek,

speaker: ADAM
text: But at fourscore, it is too late a week.

speaker: ADAM
text: Yet fortune cannot recompense me better

speaker: ADAM
text: Than to die well, and not my master’s debtor.
